item.content:encoded:
The new website looks much better than the old one. However, the Next Concert link is only showing how many days/hours until the next concert but is not showing the actual date or venue details. The actual date, time, and venue should be shown, with a map of the venue or link to a map should also be added to the page.
I also didn't realise that the empty box on the Members section was where the password was supposed to be. This needs to be labelled. However, is it really necessary to use a password to see our rehearsal schedule? If I'm out and about and would just like to look up rehearsal schedules on my phone, I may not have the pw handy so would prefer to be able to just see the rehearsal schedules without inputting any passwords. Maybe pw should just be necessary to access accounts, minutes, AGM materials?
Otherwise, looking good! Best, Grace <!--more--> AUTHOR: AUTHOR EMAIL: AUTHOR URL: SUBJECT: [WESTMINSTER PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA] New Website Feedback IP: 2.101.6.80 JSON_DATA {"1_How could this website be better?":"The new website looks much better than the old one. 
 However, the Next Concert link is only showing how many days/hours until the next concert but is not showing the actual date or venue details. The actual date, time, and venue should be shown, with a map of the venue or link to a map should also be added to the page.

I also didn't realise that the empty box on the Members section was where the password was supposed to be. This needs to be labelled. However, is it really necessary to use a password to see our rehearsal schedule? If I'm out and about and would just like to look up rehearsal schedules on my phone, I may not have the pw handy so would prefer to be able to just see the rehearsal schedules without inputting any passwords. Maybe pw should just be necessary to access accounts, minutes, AGM materials? 

Otherwise, looking good! Best, Grace","email_marketing_consent":"","entry_title":"New Website Feedback","entry_permalink":"https://wpo.org.uk/new-website-feedback/","feedback_id":"d68c9196b63a9a83b8de56438ffefff4"}

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
a:2:{s:2:"to";a:1:{i:0;s:37:"wposecretary ";}s:7:"message";s:1372:"

How could this website be better?:
The new website looks much better than the old one.
However, the Next Concert link is only showing how many days/hours until the next concert but is not showing the actual date or venue details. The actual date, time, and venue should be shown, with a map of the venue or link to a map should also be added to the page.

I also didn't realise that the empty box on the Members section was where the password was supposed to be. This needs to be labelled. However, is it really necessary to use a password to see our rehearsal schedule? If I'm out and about and would just like to look up rehearsal schedules on my phone, I may not have the pw handy so would prefer to be able to just see the rehearsal schedules without inputting any passwords. Maybe pw should just be necessary to access accounts, minutes, AGM materials?

Otherwise, looking good! Best, Grace


Time: 23rd Sep 2024 at 6:11 pm
IP Address: 2.101.6.80

Source URL: https://wpo.org.uk/new-website-feedback/

Sent by an unverified visitor to your site.

";}

item.content:encoded: Hiya, when I tried to email the link to the next concert to a friend, it is showing flyer to the May 2024 concert. Not sure what's going on...still can't see the date/time of the October concert on the Next Concert page either. item.content:encoded:
Hi Much of it looks great. A members area is all very well but it would be better if the rehearsal schedule wasn't behind a password wall so deps or people considering joining the orchestra can also see it for dates, venues, timings and upcoming repertoire. No need to have the orchestra committee's personal data on the same sheet just because it's always been that way. That IS the kind of thing that should be behind a password wall. Do we have a soloist for the concert coming up? Perhaps their name should be publicised on the page advertising the concert. Gill Bird <!--more--> AUTHOR: AUTHOR EMAIL: AUTHOR URL: SUBJECT: [WESTMINSTER PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA] New Website Feedback IP: 161.51.195.143 JSON_DATA {"1_How could this website be better?":"Hi
Much of it looks great.
A members area is all very well but it would be better if the rehearsal schedule wasn't behind a password wall so deps or people considering joining the orchestra can also see it for dates, venues, timings and upcoming repertoire. No need to have the orchestra committee's personal data on the same sheet just because it's always been that way. That IS the kind of thing that should be behind a password wall.
Do we have a soloist for the concert coming up? Perhaps their name should be publicised on the page advertising the concert.
Gill Bird","email_marketing_consent":"","entry_title":"New Website Feedback","entry_permalink":"https://wpo.org.uk/new-website-feedback/","feedback_id":"dff2183718f6402ce1f9fa3f1a013080"}

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
a:2:{s:2:"to";a:1:{i:0;s:37:"wposecretary ";}s:7:"message";s:1067:"

How could this website be better?:
Hi
Much of it looks great.
A members area is all very well but it would be better if the rehearsal schedule wasn't behind a password wall so deps or people considering joining the orchestra can also see it for dates, venues, timings and upcoming repertoire. No need to have the orchestra committee's personal data on the same sheet just because it's always been that way. That IS the kind of thing that should be behind a password wall.
Do we have a soloist for the concert coming up? Perhaps their name should be publicised on the page advertising the concert.
Gill Bird


Time: 29th Sep 2024 at 10:53 am
IP Address: 161.51.195.143

Source URL: https://wpo.org.uk/new-website-feedback/

Sent by a verified WordPress.com user.

";}

item.content:encoded:
The site looks great but you should check how it looks on a mobile rather than laptop. I’m on my phone and some words are not visible because of black background. Some words go over 2 lines e.g. Orchestr a The rehearsal schedule seems unduly long with dates, times, venues all listed separately (maybe this is just on mobile). Can’t check on laptop as password not working there. Always teething problems to deal with! Barbara <!--more--> AUTHOR: AUTHOR EMAIL: AUTHOR URL: SUBJECT: [WESTMINSTER PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA] New Website Feedback IP: 82.3.188.6 JSON_DATA {"1_How could this website be better?":"The site looks great but you should check how it looks on a mobile rather than laptop. item.dc:creator: wporepertoire
item.content:encoded: https://wpo.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/cropped-westminster_philharmonic_orchestra_logo.jpeg

Hi Meg I've been able to get into the members area on my laptop today, so that's good! For the 'next concert' page I think the background (flyer) is too messy and the colour doesn't really tone in with the rest of the page. Maybe there could be a separate 'download flyer' link? I think it would also be good to have the programme, as well as the date/time, at the top of the page. I think the less scrolling people have to do the better! Great that Buy tickets here button is at the top, but I'm not sure about the countdown clock. Seems a bit OTT perhaps? Or if others want it to stay, maybe it could be smaller? It is still difficult to read some text as it is black against a dark background e.g. New website feedback Anyway, I'm sure you are getting lots of suggestions so hope it helps. Thanks for the work you're doing. Barbara <!--more--> AUTHOR: AUTHOR EMAIL: AUTHOR URL: SUBJECT: [WESTMINSTER PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA] New Website Feedback IP: 82.3.188.6 JSON_DATA {"1_How could this website be better?":"Hi Meg
I've been able to get into the members area on my laptop today, so that's good! 
For the 'next concert' page I think the background (flyer) is too messy and the colour doesn't really tone in with the rest of the page. Maybe there could be a separate 'download flyer' link?
I think it would also be good to have the programme, as well as the date/time, at the top of the page. I think the less scrolling people have to do the better! Great that Buy tickets here button is at the top, but I'm not sure about the countdown clock. Seems a bit OTT perhaps? Or if others want it to stay, maybe it could be smaller?
It is still difficult to read some text as it is black against a dark background e.g. New website feedback
Anyway, I'm sure you are getting lots of suggestions so hope it helps.
Thanks for the work you're doing.
Barbara","email_marketing_consent":"","entry_title":"New Website Feedback","entry_permalink":"https://wpo.org.uk/new-website-feedback/","feedback_id":"c3ad4e73c7691e30c579330342694bd4"}

I've been able to get into the members area on my laptop today, so that's good!
For the 'next concert' page I think the background (flyer) is too messy and the colour doesn't really tone in with the rest of the page. Maybe there could be a separate 'download flyer' link?
I think it would also be good to have the programme, as well as the date/time, at the top of the page. I think the less scrolling people have to do the better! Great that Buy tickets here button is at the top, but I'm not sure about the countdown clock. Seems a bit OTT perhaps? Or if others want it to stay, maybe it could be smaller?
It is still difficult to read some text as it is black against a dark background e.g. New website feedback
Anyway, I'm sure you are getting lots of suggestions so hope it helps.
Thanks for the work you're doing.
Barbara


Time: 7th Oct 2024 at 10:56 am
IP Address: 82.3.188.6

Source URL: https://wpo.org.uk/new-website-feedback/

Sent by an unverified visitor to your site.

";}

item.title: About
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
• 1972

    WPO was founded by Joseph Pilbury

  • 1998

    Our Silver Anniversary was celebrated with Bartok’s Duke Bluebeard’s Castle and a commissioned work – Howard Jones’ Thamesside Variations.

  • 2002

    We recorded Scott Benzie’s music for the film Room 36 directed by Jim Groom.

  • 2003

    Our 30th Anniversary included Bernstein’s SongFest – a vocal extravaganza for six soloists and large orchestra

  • 2017

    We recorded a BT Sport ad of the UEFA Champions’ League Anthem “conducted” by Gary Lineker.

  • 2018

    Westminster Philharmonic Orchestra became a registered charity.

  • 2023

    Our 40th Anniversary included Delius’s Paris and Debussy’s complete Images, marking the joint 150th Anniversary of both composers.

item.title: Sponsor Us
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
As a charity, WPO welcomes sponsors to support our music activities.  If you run and/or manage a business and need more exposure to potential clients online and otherwise, please email us at: sponsorship@wpomail.org.uk, and we will be happy to accommodate any sponsorship offer. Your support is much appreciated.

The WPO is particularly grateful for the renewed support of Body Silk Clinic in Hither Green, London

Support us whilst browsing!

WPO has joined EasyFundraising, which means you can raise funds for the WPO every time you shop online.  To get started, install the small add-on in your web browser, create an account, then start your shopping session via the add-on.  You will be able to see the amount you have raised.

Learn More Here!
Why not become a Friend of WPO?

item.title: Our Conductor
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
Jonathan Butcher has appeared at all the major London concert venues, including the Royal Albert Hall, Barbican, South Bank and St. John’s Smith Square and has conducted orchestras including the London Mozart Players, Royal Philharmonic Concert Orchestra, English Symphony Orchestra, New London Orchestra, Guildford Philharmonic Orchestra and the London Bach Orchestra, working with international soloists including Dame Moira Lympany, Steven Isserlis, Marisa Robles, Julian Lloyd Webber and Peter Katin.

Abroad he has worked with the Promenade Orchestra of Radio Hilversum, Holland, the National Orchestra of Malta and the Staatsphilharmonie Rheinland-Pfalz.  He has conducted two Hoffnung Concerts in Munich, as well as six New Year Concerts in and around Ludvigshaven, Germany, where he has also conducted a concert of film music with the renowned jazz singer Jocelyn B. Smith.

Equally at home with the symphonic, operatic, choral and music theatre repertoire, Jonathan has conducted and presented the Family Concerts at Polesden Lacey since their inception and regularly conducts their open-air Promenade Concerts.  He was for many years Conductor and Musical Director of both the National Symphony and National Chamber Orchestras of the IAPS – the former giving their concerts in the celebrated Snape Maltings in Suffolk.

Jonathan is Musical Director of the Isle of Wight Symphony Orchestra and the Westminster Philharmonic Orchestra.  He has been Artistic Director of Surrey Opera for some thirty-five years, is co-founder and Artistic Director of The North Downs Music Group, and directs two choirs at the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art.  Jonathan is an established Director in his own right, a role he has fulfilled numerous times for Surrey Opera.

Find our more about Jonathan's work below:

Isle of Wight Symphony Orchestra

"The Isle of Wight Symphony Orchestra (IWSO) has been proud to present a regular programme of classical music, from its home venue at the Medina Theatre in Newport, for over 40 years... "

For more information, click the image below:

Surrey Opera

"Surrey Opera was founded by the late Joyce Hooper MBE in 1969. In June 1970, she embarked upon the most ambitious project of her career, staging the Mozart operas, starting with the Magic Flute..."

For more information, click the image below:

Royal Academy of Dramatic Arts

"We are dedicated to world-leading training in the dramatic arts. We offer vocational training for actors, stage managers, designers and technical stagecraft specialists..."

For more information, click the image below:

item.title: Next Concert
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
7.30pm, Saturday 28th February 2026

St John's Church, Waterloo

Holst: Ballet from The Perfect Fool

Korngold: Violin Concerto

Soloist: Charlie Lovell-Jones

George Lloyd: Symphony No. 4

Buy Tickets Here!
1772307000

  days

  hours  minutes  seconds

until

St John's Church, Waterloo

Get Directions Here!

Violin Soloist, Charlie Lovell-Jones

Charlie Lovell-Jones debuted at a sold-out Royal Festival Hall aged 15. An international soloist and critically-acclaimed concertmaster of the Sinfonia of London, he won top prizes at Oxford University and the Royal Academy of Music. He studied for eleven years with Rodney Friend MBE, and two years with Augustin Hadelich at the Yale School of Music. He has many competition successes, and enjoys a busy international concert and recording schedule. He treasures opportunities to connect with audiences, and enjoys exploring classical music's 21st-Century capabilities. Charlie plays a fine 1777 G.B. Guadagnini violin on loan from a generous benefactor.

Read more about Charlie here.

Frank Noon Photography, December 2021

item.title: Season 2023-24
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:

St John's Waterloo

St John's Waterloo

item.title: History
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
The Westminster Philharmonic Orchestra was founded in 1972 by Joseph Pilbury for musicians of the highest standard to perform large-scale works including the symphonies of Mahler and Shostakovich. We have built an impressive reputation as one of London’s leading amateur orchestras, bringing together players from a wide geographical area for music-making, covering a broad range of music from the 19th century onwards.

Major milestones in the WPO’s repertoire include Bartok’s Miraculous Mandarin, Ravel's Daphnis & Chloé, the full cycle of Mahler symphonies, Rachmaninoff’s three symphonies, Stravinsky’s The Rite of Spring and Firebird, Tippett's Ritual Dances, Maxwell Davis' Five Klee Pictures and Reel with Northern Lights, Shostakovich Symphonies 5, 6, 8, 10, 11 and 12, the Elgar/Payne Symphony no.3, Nielsen's 5th Symphony, Strauss’ Also Sprach Zarathustra and Vaughan Williams’ 6th Symphony.

Our Silver Anniversary in 1998 was celebrated with Bartok’s Duke Bluebeard’s Castle and a commissioned work - Howard Jones’ Thamesside Variations. Our 30th Anniversary included Bernstein’s SongFest - a vocal extravaganza for six soloists and large orchestra - and our 40th, Delius’s Paris and Debussy’s complete Images, marking the joint 150th Anniversary of both composers.

Under the direction of our principal Conductor, Jonathan Butcher, we have worked with many distinguished soloists, in particular Raphael Wallfisch for the Finzi Concerto and Prokofiev’s Sinfonia Concertante, and, in collaboration with music trusts, have offered concerto opportunities to several outstanding young soloists at the start of their international careers.

The WPO is keen to promote rarely heard and new works: in 2012 we celebrated the centenary of Samuel Coleridge-Taylor with his Violin Concerto and Hiawatha’s Wedding Feast; and marked the 2013 centenary of George Lloyd with his Cello Concerto. In 2018 we gave the public premiere of David Hackbridge Johnson’s 9th Symphony, previously recorded to considerable critical acclaim, and in March 2019, the Concerto for violin & cello by Percy Sherwood. In 2018 we celebrated Leonard Bernstein’s centenary presenting a complete programme of his music including The Age of Anxiety (Symphony No.2), Serenade on Plato’s Symposium and a WPO favourite, the Symphonic Dances from West Side Story.

In addition to appearing in all the major concert halls of London we have toured abroad to destinations including Gibraltar, Malta, Cyprus, France, Spain, Italy, the Czech Republic and Nova Scotia. Our annual “Party in the Park” Concert at Langtons Gardens, Hornchurch continues to attract audiences of over 3,000 people.

The WPO has released 4 CDs of film music on the Silva Screen label: Franz Waxman’s ‘The Bride of Frankenstein’ (often aired on BBC Radio 3); Max Steiner’s Gone With The Wind, ‘Horror’ and ‘The Devil Rides Out’ and The Great British Film Music Album. In 2002 we recorded Scott Benzie’s music for the film Room 36 directed by Jim Groom.

More unusually, in 2017 we recorded a BT Sport ad of the UEFA Champions’ League Anthem “conducted” by Gary Lineker.

item.title: Friends
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
The Friends of the Westminster Philharmonic gives our supporters the opportunity to contribute a little more to the continuing support and development of the orchestra and allows us to maintain our enterprising programmes, matching rare, unusual and less-frequently played works with familiar pieces from the repertoire.

In the present climate, funding for any arts organisation becomes increasingly difficult. The WPO is keen to maintain its adventurous approach and to provide players and audiences an opportunity to perform and hear music not often programmed elsewhere.

This adventurousness needs support however: ticket sales cover barely a quarter of the cost of putting on a concert; we need to cover the funding gap and this is where our Friends can help.

If you are interested in supporting us further, or for any form of corporate support, please contact our Friends Administrator: friends@wpo.org.uk. Thank you.

Friends receive a newsletter, a free programme and an interval drink at concerts. 

Current Friends of the WPO include:-

  • Carys Jones
  • Ali and Dick Warn
  • Adrian and Jean Jones
  • Rosemary Clarke-Jones
  • Michael and Ursula Kellett
  • Jo Knorpel
  • Nathan Blackwell

item.title: Our Leader
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
Christian started learning the violin with his mother Lucy when he was 5 years old. He continued his studies with Dona Lee Croft at both the RCM Junior Dept and later as an undergraduate of the Royal College of Music, where he won several prizes (including the concerto award for his performance of The Lark Ascending, which he subsequently performed with RCM Sinfonietta in the presence of Lady Ursula Vaughan-Williams).

Since graduating in 1999, Christian has enjoyed a busy and varied career as a freelance violinist, working with ensembles such as London Mozart Players, St. Paul’s Sinfonia, City Chamber Orchestra, 18th Century Concert Orchestra, Ex Cathedra, and Armonico Consort.

Christian was a founder member of the Southbank Sinfonia in 2002-3; he was appointed leader of Kent Sinfonia in December 2011.

He also leads Surrey Opera, Westminster Philharmonic Orchestra, Royal Orchestral Society and the Whitehall Orchestra, with whom he recently performed Prokofiev 2nd violin concerto. Christian has also been guest-leader for London Phoenix Orchestra, Trinity Orchestra Harrow, Epping Symphony Orchestra and the Isle of Wight Symphony Orchestra.

Passionate about chamber music, Christian has been 1st violin with the Abraxas Ensemble since 2000 and was 2nd violin with the Billroth Quartet 2012-17.

item.content:encoded:

WPOMinutesJan26Download

11th February 2025

Shortened Summary (link to full notes at the end):

Key Decisions & Discussions:

  • Program Suggestions: Meg will compile suggestions; Frank will check cost and past performances.
  • Role Changes: Frank stepping down as librarian & first trumpet (after 36 years). Search for replacements underway.
  • Concert/Rehearsal Dates: Adjustments needed to avoid Easter conflicts; first & last concerts at St. Giles.
  • Islington Choral Society Issues: Concerns over professional players being hired without consultation. Negotiations planned.
  • Pricing Adjustments: Subscription at £30/£50; door prices increasing from £10 to £12. Online sales may close earlier.
  • Bassoon Section: No current principal bassoonist.
  • Wagner Tuba Acquisition: Four secured from ENO; rental is £50 each.
  • Making Music Subsidy: £250 received from Young Artists Scheme.
  • Donation in Memory of Moira: £500 to fund a professional cellist; Joseph Spooner suggested.
  • AGM Planning: Set for April 27, including concert schedule, finances, and a potential event for Jonathan’s 40th year.
  • Other Business:
    • Piano booked for Bernstein piece; Derek may play.

Next Meeting: March 4 at 19:00.

250211 Committee Meeting NotesDownload
14th January 2025

Shortened Summary (link to full notes at the end):

Heating: Resolved, extended to 6 PM.Woodwind Rehearsal (26th Jan): Possible early start, £50 extra cost, pending approval.Concert Dates 2025-26:

  • 6th Dec & 28th Feb – Saint John’s Waterloo
  • 9th May – St Giles Cripplegate
  • Rehearsal dates TBC.

Islington Choral Society (ICS) Collaboration: £3,500 fee for "The Kingdom", one rehearsal planned, possible extra.Program Suggestions: 47 received, deadline end of Jan. More diversity needed.Players:

  • Bassoon: Jeff Roussel covering next concert, long-term options considered.
  • Trombones: Mark Horton to play principal.
  • Pianist: Derek to book.
  • Wagner Tubas (Bruckner 9): To be sourced, needed 27th Apr & 3rd May.

Finances: Balance £9,021, expected income from Gift Aid (£1,500) & OTR (£6,000).£500 Donation: To fund a cello soloist (Tchaikovsky Rococo Variations).Tickets & Membership:

  • Friends: £30 individual, £50 couple.
  • Concessions: £10 advance, £12 door.
  • ICS members offered concession rates.

Next Meeting: 11th Feb 2025.AGM: Proposed 13th Apr 2025

250114 Committee Meeting NotesDownload
2nd September 2024

Shortened Summary (link to full notes at the end):

Attendance: Frank, Adrian, Derek, Nathan, Alexia, Dominic, Meg
Apologies: Dug

Jonathan’s Health

  • Jonathan has been unwell but is responding to treatment. Discussed potential succession planning, mentioning Orlando Joplin, Adrian Brown, and Mark Dooley as options. A contingency plan is suggested.

Next Season

  • Kijé: Decided not to include a baritone soloist; will explore saxophone option instead.
  • Concert Soloist: Alexia has secured a pianist for one future concert.
  • Venue: Derek to book a large piano and stands at St John’s Waterloo.
  • Harpists: Frank is managing arrangements.
240902 Committee Meeting NotesDownload
17th January 2024

Shortened Summary (link to full notes at the end):

Last Meeting Highlights:

  • Next Season’s Dates: Adrian will circulate dates for 2024/25.
  • Rehearsal Timings: Adrian to discuss scheduling options with Jonathan.
  • Repertoire: A curated list sent to Jonathan for 6-8 program selections.
  • Website Updates: Meg to develop a combined form for Gift Aid/Registration and explore member-only features.
  • Heating KSA: Discount on the next bill noted.
  • JB Booking Violins Update: Clarification on payments and committee approvals; all future payments to be approved.
  • 40th Anniversary Planning: Ideas needed to celebrate JB's anniversary in October; Frank has a repertoire list from 1984.

Current Meeting Items:

  • Apologies from Derek.
  • Fee Adjustments: Additional fee approved for Christian's Heldenleben part; Adrian to confirm Menhuin fee of £250 with new Chair.
  • Conductor and Leader Fees Review: Discussion on fee increases, with the last adjustment being from £1000 to £1200. Adrian to communicate intentions for review by September 2024.
  • Financial Overview: Current balance is £8688; potential to recover £2,000 through Gift Aid.
  • Zettle Machine: One machine sufficient for now.
  • Bassoon Section: Trial players engaged; interest from others noted.
  • Soloists for Upcoming Concerts: Two pianists considered.
  • Post-Concert Cleanup: Proposal for alternating cleanup responsibilities among sections.
  • String Player Recruitment: Meg to reach out to conservatoires for additional string players.
  • Social Media Promotions: Adrian to gain Facebook admin control; Meg to explore Instagram and TikTok.
  • Ticket Pricing: Consideration to raise door prices and potentially eliminate cash payments.
  • AGM Date: Set for April 21, 2024.
  • Next Meeting: Scheduled for March 5, 2024, at 19:30, with a push for Jonathan’s program selection.

Action Items:

  • Adrian to circulate 2024/25 dates and confirm Menhuin fee.
  • Meg to develop website forms and recruit string players.
  • Adrian to review conductor and leader fees with the committee.
  • Follow up on social media and ticket pricing strategies.
240110 Committee Meeting NotesDownload
21st November 2023

Shortened Summary (link to full notes at the end):

Agenda:

  1. Next Season Schedule:
    • One concert in 2024, followed by three concerts in 2025, due to time pressures around Christmas.
    • Rehearsal and concert dates for 2024/25 proposed, awaiting Jonathan’s confirmation.
    • No date for Havering yet—Derek to follow up with Jonathan.
  2. Repertoire:
    • Alexia to gather suggestions from the orchestra to encourage member involvement in programme selection.
  3. Website:
    • New website progress: committee feedback needed.
    • Meg to explore payment portals for subs and donations, a members-only login, and diversity initiatives with other amateur orchestras.
  4. JB Booking Violins:
    • Jonathan apologized for booking soloists without consulting the committee.
    • Meg to handle fee-related matters and be the designated safeguarding officer.
  5. Rehearsal Timings:
    • Decision to permanently shift rehearsal timings to 15:00–18:00 to avoid paying for unused time. This will apply to the upcoming St. Giles Christmas Concert.
  6. Bassoons:
    • Trying out a new principal bassoonist for this concert. Further candidates to be tested, with the principal bassoon responsible for filling section roles once selected.
  7. Heating at KSA:
    • Ongoing heating issues during rehearsals. A possible discount on the next hire season was offered, though no long-term fix is in place.
  8. Principal Violin:
    • Christian’s request for a higher fee due to a challenging part was approved, with an additional £100 to be paid.
  9. Financial Update:
    • Havering invoice submitted (£3,000). Gift Aid for last year totaled over £8,000.
    • Nathan to finalize Gift Aid and focus next on Orchestra Tax Relief.
  10. AOB:
    • Discussion on Jonathan’s 40th season.
    • Facebook admin rights to be transferred from Jemma to Dug.
    • Website amendments are needed for Adrian’s WPO email.

Next meeting: January 17, 2024, at 19:00.

231121 Committee Meeting NotesDownload
21st November 2023

Shortened Summary (link to full notes at the end):

Agenda:

  1. Soloist for Saint-Saëns:
    • A soloist from the Menuhin School has been secured by Jonathan, with details to follow. Meg is coordinating rehearsal dates.
  2. Return of Gipps Parts:
    • Missing parts have been returned, no additional costs incurred.
  3. Website Redesign:
    • Meg and Frank to fully transfer the website to WordPress due to issues with the current host.
  4. String Numbers:
    • Low response to Barbara’s email on player availability, but Rojer Livid will join cellos for the first concert.
    • 11 violas signed up for the season, which is a positive.
  5. Bass Players:
    • A shortage of double bass players for the season may require extra budget.
    • Meg will reach out to ex-Guildhall and Trinity Laban students, and Derek will share contact info with Dominic.
  6. First Bassoon:
    • Derek has a potential contact for a bassoonist and will pass details to Adrian.
  7. Rehearsal Conflicts:
    • Rehearsals are scheduled during half-term weekends, causing complaints. No changes for this season, but the issue will be considered in future planning.
  8. Subs for Retired Members:
    • No formal discount for retired members, though a previous 50% discount for students could be applied. Nathan reported healthy finances, so subs remain unchanged.
    • Nathan is working on recovering Gift Aid and requires updated membership and subs data from Alexia.
  9. Leader:
    • Christian is unavailable for the first two concerts and has arranged for James Widden to replace him. An increased fee for the violin concerto was agreed upon.
  10. Friends of the Orchestra:
    • A newsletter will be sent to both Friends and members, encouraging more people to become Friends. This helps keep subscription costs down.

Other Business (AOB):

  • John Kendrick’s Passing: A card will be arranged in memory of John Kendrick, a violinist with the orchestra.
  • Programming: The committee discussed inviting string section leaders to provide input on the programming draft.
  • Health Notice: A reminder about the recent rise in COVID-19 cases.
  • Next Meeting: Scheduled for November 7, 2023, at 19:00.

This meeting covered key logistical issues, upcoming concert planning, and discussions about personnel and financial health.

230905 Committee Meeting NotesDownload

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
Welcome!

The WPO is one of London’s leading amateur orchestras, playing the biggest and most demanding works of the last 150 years

Join us for a bold and richly varied season of orchestral masterworks, rare gems, and electrifying solo performances. Across five exciting concerts in some of London’s most atmospheric venues, the Westminster Philharmonic Orchestra invites you to experience a musical journey through drama, colour, and imagination.

See our full 2025-26 Season

Our next concert is on 28th February 2026.

BUY TICKETS HERE!

Why not join WPO?

The WPO rehearses on Sundays in central London.  We welcome experienced amateur musicians.
Fill out the form and we will be in touch!!

Join the Orchestra!

item.title: Join WPO
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
Join WPO

The WPO rehearses on Sundays in central London.  Our conductor is Jonathan Butcher.  We welcome experienced amateur musicians and we have particular vacancies at the moment for violins, violas, cellos and double basses, though everyone is welcome. 

Contact Us

item.title: Collaborative Concerts
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
Next Collaborative Concert with Islington Choral Society:

7.00pm, Sunday 6th April 2025

Sinfonia Smith Square, SW1P 3HA

Get Directions Here!

Elgar, The Kingdom

Buy Tickets Here!

About Islington Choral Society

"Islington Choral Society is a lively, friendly, non-auditioned performing group of choral singers based in Islington, north London.

Our musical repertoire is varied, and includes some of the most famous, fun and challenging choral pieces.

We usually perform 3 concerts a year: one towards the end of each school term.  

We currently rehearse on Tuesday nights (7.30pm to 9.30pm) at Highbury Roundhouse, 71 Ronalds Rd, London N5 1XB"

To learn more about Islington Choral Society and their upcoming concerts, click here.

item.title: Season 2025-26
item.dc:creator: wposecretary
item.content:encoded:
Westminster Philharmonic Orchestra hold five concerts each season, taking place at St John's Waterloo, St Giles Cripplegate, and Langton's Lakeside.

We are also honoured to work closely with other organisations throughout the year, including our frequent collabrotive partner, Islington Choral Society.

Our season opens on Saturday 18 October 2025 at St Giles Cripplegate, with a colourful and rhythmically charged programme. Poulenc’s witty and effervescent Les Biches sets the tone, followed by Edward Gregson’s powerful Tuba Concerto, with soloist to be announced. The mystical landscape of Sibelius’s En Saga leads us into the brilliant and fiery world of Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite (1919 version), rounding out an evening of rich orchestral colour and expressive range.

WPO Concert 1:
Saturday 18th October 2025 7:30pm
St Giles Cripplegate

Poulenc: Les Biches

Gregson: Tuba Concerto

Soloist: Andrew Kershaw

Sibelius: En Saga

Stravinsky: Firebird Suite

On Saturday 6 December 2025, we return to St John’s Waterloo for a concert full of drama, lyricism, and English musical heritage. Samuel Coleridge-Taylor’s sparkling The Bamboula opens the evening, leading into Rachmaninoff’s beloved Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini, showcasing a solo pianist (t.b.a.) in a dazzling display of virtuosity. The programme concludes with Ralph Vaughan Williams’s majestic and deeply spiritual Job: A Masque for Dancing, a powerful evocation of suffering, faith, and redemption.

WPO Concert 2:
Saturday 6th December 2025
7:30pm
St John's Waterloo

Coleridge-Taylor: The Bamboula

Rachmaninoff: Paganini Rhapsody

Soloist: Agnieszka Pluta

Vaughan Williams: Job: A Masque for Dancing

The orchestra’s third concert, on Saturday 28 February 2026 at St John’s Waterloo, blends ballet, cinematic romance, and symphonic sweep. Holst’s flamboyant Ballet Music from The Perfect Fool opens the evening with otherworldly flair, followed by Erich Korngold’s lush and emotionally charged Violin Concerto, performed by the thrilling young soloist Charlie Lovell-Jones. The programme ends with George Lloyd’s monumental Symphony No. 4, an expansive and rarely heard work of remarkable depth and grandeur.

WPO Concert 3:
Saturday 28th February 2026
7:30pm
St John's Waterloo

Holst: Ballet from The Perfect Fool

Korngold: Violin Concerto

Soloist: Charlie Lovell-Jones

George Lloyd: Symphony No. 4

Our final concert of the main season takes place on Saturday 9 May 2026 at St Giles Cripplegate, with a spring-themed programme that highlights innovation and intensity. Lili Boulanger’s shimmering miniature D’un matin de printemps offers a radiant start, followed by Bantock’s evocative Russian Scenes. The spotlight then turns to a rare performance of Ruth Gipps’s lyrical and beautifully crafted Oboe Concerto (soloist t.b.a.). The season concludes with Elgar’s Symphony No. 1, a masterwork of nobility, pathos, and English symphonic brilliance.

WPO Concert 4:
Saturday 9th May 2026
7:30pm
St St Giles Cripplegate

Lili Boulanger: D'un Matin de Printemps

Bantock: Russian Scenes

Gipps: Oboe Concerto

Soloist: Ewan Millar

Elgar: Symphony No. 1

We round off the year with a summer outdoor concert on Sunday 7 June 2026 at Langton’s Lakeside in Havering (to be confirmed). This relaxed, family-friendly event promises a joyful mix of light classics and seasonal favourites in a beautiful open-air setting. Full programme details will be announced closer to the date.

WPO Concert 5 TBC:
Sunday 7th June 2026 TBC
3:00pm
Langton's Lakeside, Havering

Repertoire tba.
